ogTitle: Ferny Grove vs Ficks Crossing: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Ferny Grove and Ficks Crossing.
heading: Ferny Grove vs Ficks Crossing — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ferny Grove, Queensland and Ficks Crossing, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ficks Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Ferny Grove (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ferny Grove or Ficks Crossing safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ferny Grove has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Ficks Crossing scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ferny Grove scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Ficks Crossing scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ferny Grove or Ficks Crossing?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ficks Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
